summary: No-ship: happy paths are covered, but material edge cases remain in live-session liveness, internal filtering, title correctness, and history scaling.

findings.title: Stale registry rows can survive PID reuse
findings.recommendation: Do not treat process existence alone as proof that the registry row still belongs to the Claude session. At minimum validate registry freshness via updatedAt/startedAt, match the filename PID to the JSON PID, and consider a heartbeat TTL or process-start-time check where available.

findings.title: Exact ~/.garrison cwd is not treated as internal
findings.recommendation: Normalize cwd and explicitly reject cwd === path.join(home, '.garrison') in addition to descendants. Add a regression test for the exact ~/.garrison root.

findings.title: History title is not actually the latest ai-title
findings.recommendation: Either scan enough of the file to prove the latest ai-title, or make the bounded sampling safe by reading complete JSONL records around the tail boundary and documenting/fallback-handling unsampled gaps instead of silently preferring an older head title or first-user snippet.

findings.title: listHistory still does unbounded filesystem work per call
findings.recommendation: Bound the number of project dirs/files visited or use a streaming top-K approach with a hard scan budget. The current limit only caps content reads after every candidate has already been listed, statted, stored, and globally sorted.

findings.title: History cache is process-lifetime and unbounded
findings.recommendation: Add an LRU/size cap and prune entries for deleted or out-of-window transcripts. Avoid caching transient null reads indefinitely on the same mtime key.
